questions.question: What is a Database?

What is a table in a database?
options: A collection of related data organized in rows and columns

questions.question: What is a row in a database table?
What is a column in a database table?
What is a primary key?
options: A unique identifier for a record

questions.question: What is a foreign key?
options: A key that references a primary key in another table
A unique identifier for a record

questions.question: What is a composite key?
options: A key that consists of two or more columns
A unique identifier for a record

questions.question: What is a candidate key?
options: A column or set of columns that can uniquely identify a record
A unique identifier for a record

questions.question: What is an alternate key?
options: A candidate key that is not the primary key
A unique identifier for a record

questions.question: What is a super key?
options: A set of columns that can uniquely identify a record
A unique identifier for a record

questions.question: What is normalization?
options: The process of organizing data to reduce redundancy

questions.question: What is the first normal form (1NF)?
options: A table is in 1NF if it has no repeating groups
A table is in 1NF if it has no duplicate rows
A table is in 1NF if it has a primary key
A table is in 1NF if it has foreign keys

questions.question: What is the second normal form (2NF)?
options: A table is in 2NF if it is in 1NF and all non-key attributes are fully dependent on the primary key
A table is in 2NF if it has no repeating groups
A table is in 2NF if it has a primary key
A table is in 2NF if it has foreign keys

questions.question: What is the third normal form (3NF)?
options: A table is in 3NF if it is in 2NF and has no transitive dependencies
A table is in 3NF if it is in 1NF and all non-key attributes are fully dependent on the primary key
A table is in 3NF if it has no repeating groups
A table is in 3NF if it has a primary key

questions.question: What is BCNF?
What is denormalization?
options: The process of adding redundancy to improve performance
The process of organizing data to reduce redundancy

questions.question: What is a query in DBMS?
options: A request for data from a database

options: A statement used to retrieve data from a database
A statement used to insert data into a database
A statement used to update data in a database
A statement used to delete data from a database

A statement used to retrieve data from a database

A statement used to delete data from a database

A statement used to insert data into a database

A statement used to delete data from a database

A statement used to insert data into a database
A statement used to retrieve data from a database
A statement used to update data in a database

questions.question: What is the CREATE statement in SQL?
options: A statement used to create tables or databases
A statement used to insert data into a database
A statement used to retrieve data from a database
A statement used to delete data from a database

questions.question: What is the ALTER statement in SQL?
options: A statement used to modify the structure of a table
A statement used to create tables or databases
A statement used to insert data into a database
A statement used to retrieve data from a database

questions.question: What is the DROP statement in SQL?
options: A statement used to delete tables or databases
A statement used to modify the structure of a table
A statement used to create tables or databases
A statement used to insert data into a database

questions.question: What is the TRUNCATE statement in SQL?
options: A statement used to remove all rows from a table
A statement used to delete tables or databases
A statement used to modify the structure of a table
A statement used to create tables or databases

questions.question: What is the WHERE clause in SQL?
options: A clause used to filter records
A clause used to sort records
A clause used to group records
A clause used to join tables

questions.question: What is the ORDER BY clause in SQL?
options: A clause used to sort records
A clause used to filter records
A clause used to group records
A clause used to join tables

questions.question: What is the GROUP BY clause in SQL?
options: A clause used to group records
A clause used to sort records
A clause used to filter records
A clause used to join tables

questions.question: What is the HAVING clause in SQL?
options: A clause used to filter groups
A clause used to group records
A clause used to sort records
A clause used to filter records

questions.question: What is a join in SQL?
options: A way to combine data from two or more tables

questions.question: What is an INNER JOIN in SQL?
options: A join that returns records that have matching values in both tables
A join that returns all records from the left table
A join that returns all records from the right table
A join that returns all records from both tables

questions.question: What is a LEFT JOIN in SQL?
options: A join that returns all records from the left table
A join that returns records that have matching values in both tables
A join that returns all records from the right table
A join that returns all records from both tables

questions.question: What is a RIGHT JOIN in SQL?
options: A join that returns all records from the right table
A join that returns all records from the left table
A join that returns records that have matching values in both tables
A join that returns all records from both tables

questions.question: What is a FULL OUTER JOIN in SQL?
options: A join that returns all records from both tables
A join that returns all records from the left table
A join that returns all records from the right table
A join that returns records that have matching values in both tables

questions.question: What is a CROSS JOIN in SQL?
options: A join that returns all combinations of rows from both tables
A join that returns all records from the left table
A join that returns all records from the right table
A join that returns records that have matching values in both tables

questions.question: What is a self-join in SQL?
options: A join that joins a table to itself
A join that returns all records from the left table
A join that returns all records from the right table
A join that returns records that have matching values in both tables

questions.question: What is an index in a database?
options: A data structure that improves the speed of data retrieval

questions.question: What is a clustered index?
options: An index that determines the physical order of data
An index that does not determine the physical order of data

questions.question: What is a non-clustered index?
options: An index that does not determine the physical order of data
An index that determines the physical order of data

questions.question: What is a view in SQL?
options: A virtual table based on the result set of a query

questions.question: What is a stored procedure in SQL?
options: A prepared SQL code that can be saved and reused

questions.question: What is a trigger in SQL?
options: A stored procedure that automatically executes in response to an event
A prepared SQL code that can be saved and reused

options: A transaction is treated as a single unit

options: A transaction is treated as a single unit

questions.question: What is Durability in DBMS?
options: A transaction is treated as a single unit
